functional_limitations: Difficulty ambulating distances greater than 200 feet

denial_risk_flags.label: No Completed Imaging Prior to Submission
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Both X-ray and MRI results are absent — the X-ray ordered at 01/30/2025 is pending and MRI has not been ordered. United Healthcare requires completed imaging with documented findings before authorizing CPT 27130.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Do not submit the prior authorization until the X-ray results are available and documented in the chart. Consider ordering and completing an MRI as well to strengthen the clinical record.

denial_risk_flags.label: Imaging Not Yet Complete
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Payers require completed imaging results before authorizing surgical procedures. The chart explicitly states the X-ray is pending and MRI has not been ordered.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Do not submit until imaging results are available and documented.

denial_risk_flags.label: Insufficient Conservative Care
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Only 1 treatment documented (OTC pain medication with no duration). Payers for CPT 27130 typically require documented failure of physical therapy (minimum 6 weeks), NSAIDs, and at least one injection before approving surgical intervention.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Obtain records documenting additional conservative treatments or initiate and document further conservative care before submission.

denial_risk_flags.label: No Physical Therapy Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The referral explicitly notes 'No formal physical therapy documented.' United Healthcare typically requires a minimum of 6 weeks of supervised physical therapy as a prerequisite for CPT 27130 approval.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Initiate and document a formal physical therapy program of at least 6 weeks duration, or obtain prior PT records if therapy was completed elsewhere, before submitting the prior authorization.

denial_risk_flags.label: OTC Medication — No Duration or Outcome Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The only conservative treatment documented is OTC pain medication with no duration, specific medication name, dosage, or outcome recorded. Payers require specific documentation of treatment trials including duration and response.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Addend the chart to specify the medication name (e.g., ibuprofen, naproxen), dosage, duration of use, and the patient's response or reason for failure.

denial_risk_flags.label: No Injection Therapy Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: No corticosteroid or hyaluronic acid injections are documented. United Healthcare guidelines for CPT 27130 commonly require at least one documented injection trial prior to surgical approval.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Document whether injection therapy was attempted, refused, or contraindicated. If not yet attempted, consider a corticosteroid injection trial and document the outcome before submission.

denial_risk_flags.label: Vague Symptom Duration — No Specific Timeframe
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Symptom duration is documented only as 'over the past several months,' which does not establish a specific timeline. Payers may require a minimum documented symptom duration to justify surgical intervention.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Addend the chart to specify the exact onset date or number of months the patient has experienced left hip pain to establish a clear clinical timeline.

denial_risk_flags.label: Preliminary Chart — Pre-op Workup Incomplete
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The chart explicitly states this is 'preliminary referral documentation' with 'full pre-op workup pending.' Submitting a prior authorization on an incomplete chart increases the risk of denial due to insufficient medical necessity documentation.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Complete the pre-operative workup including imaging, ASA classification, pain scoring, and objective measurements before submitting the prior authorization to United Healthcare.

denial_risk_flags.label: No Surgical Approach Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: No surgical approach (e.g., anterior, posterior, lateral) is specified in the chart. While not always required for authorization, some payers request this detail to confirm appropriateness of the planned procedure.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Addend the operative plan to specify the intended surgical approach for the left total hip arthroplasty.

denial_risk_flags.label: No Pain Score Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: No numeric pain scale rating (VAS, NRS) is documented in the chart. Pain scores are commonly required by payers to substantiate functional impairment and medical necessity.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Document a baseline pain score using a standardized scale (e.g., NRS 0–10) at rest and with activity at the next clinical encounter.

diagnosis_codes.note: ICD-10 code M16.12 (primary osteoarthritis, left hip) is explicitly documented and is appropriate for CPT 27130.

conservative_treatments_named.note: Only one vague conservative treatment (OTC pain medication) is named with no specifics; physical therapy and injections are absent.

conservative_treatment_duration.note: No duration is documented for any conservative treatment attempted.

imaging_findings.note: No imaging results are available; X-ray is pending and MRI has not been ordered.

functional_limitations.note: Two specific functional limitations are documented: ambulation limited to 200 feet and inability to perform prolonged standing.

surgical_approach.note: No surgical approach is documented for the planned left total hip arthroplasty.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 27130 (total hip arthroplasty) is explicitly stated and aligns with the diagnosis of left hip osteoarthritis (M16.12).

symptom_duration.note: Symptom duration is vague ('over the past several months') with no specific onset date or number of months documented.

hard_blocks.label: Imaging Findings
hard_blocks.message: No imaging results are documented. Payers require objective imaging evidence to authorize surgical intervention.

soft_warnings.label: Surgical Approach
soft_warnings.message: Anatomical approach details strengthen the surgical indication but can be inferred.
